g.title: Shaft element based on Timoshenko beam theory. Models shaft segments with 4 DOF per node (translation x, y and rotation θx, θy). Supports cylindrical and tapered sections.
L: element length [m] idl: left inner diameter [m] odl: left outer diameter [m] idr: right inner diameter [m] odr: right outer diameter [m] material: Material object (E, G, ρ)
Includes shear effects (Timoshenko) Default: True If False, behaves as Euler-Bernoulli beam
Includes rotary inertia in mass matrix Default: True
Includes gyroscopic effect (G matrix) Default: True Fundamental for rotor analysis
Axial force on element [N] Modifies lateral stiffness (stress stiffening)
Torque applied to element [N·m] Affects stability and mode coupling
Mass matrix [8×8] Translational mass + rotary inertia Includes tapered section effects if applicable
Stiffness matrix [8×8] Bending + shear (if enabled) Includes stress stiffening (if axial_force ≠ 0)
Damping matrix [8×8] Proportional damping (Rayleigh) or null matrix if not specified
Gyroscopic matrix [8×8] Coupling between xz and yz planes Proportional to rotational speed
ShaftElement(L, idl, odl, idr, odr, material, ...) Specifies all parameters directly
Creates multiple ShaftElements from a table (DataFrame) with the dimensions of each shaft section. Useful for importing geometries from spreadsheets or files.
Constant cross-section along the length idl = idr and odl = odr Most common case for uniform shafts
Variable cross-section along the length idl ≠ idr or odl ≠ odr For transitions and shaft shoulders
Shaft element based on Timoshenko beam theory. Models shaft segments with 4 DOF per node (translation x, y and rotation θx, θy). Supports cylindrical and tapered sections.
L: element length [m] idl: left inner diameter [m] odl: left outer diameter [m] idr: right inner diameter [m] odr: right outer diameter [m] material: Material object (E, G, ρ)
Includes shear effects (Timoshenko) Default: True If False, behaves as Euler-Bernoulli beam
Includes rotary inertia in mass matrix Default: True
Includes gyroscopic effect (G matrix) Default: True Fundamental for rotor analysis
Axial force on element [N] Modifies lateral stiffness (stress stiffening)
Torque applied to element [N·m] Affects stability and mode coupling
Mass matrix [8×8] Translational mass + rotary inertia Includes tapered section effects if applicable
Stiffness matrix [8×8] Bending + shear (if enabled) Includes stress stiffening (if axial_force ≠ 0)
Damping matrix [8×8] Proportional damping (Rayleigh) or null matrix if not specified
Gyroscopic matrix [8×8] Coupling between xz and yz planes Proportional to rotational speed
ShaftElement(L, idl, odl, idr, odr, material, ...) Specifies all parameters directly
Creates multiple ShaftElements from a table (DataFrame) with the dimensions of each shaft section. Useful for importing geometries from spreadsheets or files.
Constant cross-section along the length idl = idr and odl = odr Most common case for uniform shafts
Variable cross-section along the length idl ≠ idr or odl ≠ odr For transitions and shaft shoulders
